about: Bank of Baroda is engaged in providing various services, such as personal banking, corporate banking, international banking, small and medium enterprise (SME) banking, rural banking, non-resident Indian (NRI) services and treasury services.(Source : Company Web-site)

key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

Bank of Baroda is engaged in providing various services, such as personal banking, corporate banking, international banking, small and medium enterprise (SME) banking, rural banking, non-resident Indian (NRI) services and treasury services.(Source : Company Web-site)

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Market Position
The bank is among India’s top five banks by asset size and total deposits with a 6% market share as of FY24. [1]

Revenue Mix
Retail Banking: 39% in Q3 FY26 vs 35% in FY22
Wholesale Banking: 32% in Q3 FY26 vs 31% in FY22
Treasury: 21% in Q3 FY26 vs 29% in FY22
Others: 8% in Q3 FY26 vs 5% in FY22 [2][3]

Key Ratios
CRAR: 16.47% in Q3 FY26 vs 16.31% in FY22[4]
GNPA: 2.04% in Q3 FY26 vs 6.61% in FY22
NNPA: 0.57% in Q3 FY26 vs 1.72% in FY22
PCR: 92.73% in Q3 FY26 vs 88.71% in FY22 [5]
Yield on Advances: 7.56% in Q3 FY26 vs 6.79% in FY22
Cost of Deposits: 4.75% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.52% in FY22
NIM: 2.79% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.03% in FY22 [6][7]

Branch Network[8]
As of Q3FY 26, the bank operates in 8,500 branches, 11,563 ATMs, 7,546 Self service passbooks, 2,034 Cash Recyclers. Overall digital touch points were 19,185 with 1148 Cr transactions.

Branch Break-Up FY25:[9]
Rural: 35%
Semi-Urban: 26%
Metro: 21.5%
Urban: 18%

Advances[10]
The gross domestic advances of the bank were Rs. 13,44,904 Cr as of Q3 FY26 vs Rs. 11,73,034 Cr as of Q3 FY25.

Advance Mix:[10]
Corporate: 38%
Retail: 31%
Agriculture: 16%
MSME: 15%

Deposits[11]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank has total domestic deposits of Rs. 15,46,749 Cr compared to Rs. 14,02,909 Cr in Q3 FY25.

Deposits Mix:[11]
Term Deposits: 62%
Savings Deposits: 32%
Current Deposits: 6%

International Business[8]
The Bank has 80 overseas offices across 15 countries including 37 overseas branches, 1 IBU in GIFT City, Gujarat, and 9 EBSUs in UAE, 52 branches of the Bank’s 7 overseas subsidiaries. In addition, It has one Joint Venture, India International Bank in Malaysia, and one associate bank, Indo Zambia Bank Ltd. in Zambia with 32 branches. The international business accounts for 18% of the gross advances and 15% of the total deposits as of Q3 FY26. [12][10][11]

Digital Initiatives
The bank offers digital banking solutions like BoB World, BoB World UPI, Baroda Connect, Debit Cards, Prepaid Cards, BHIM Aadhaar, ATM and Cash Recycler machines, Self Service Passbook Printers, TAB Banking, etc. As of Q3 FY26, 96% of the bank's transactions are done digitally. It is ranked 2nd in terms of debit card issuance and 3rd in terms of UPI remittance. [13][14]

Regulatory Ban
In October 2023, the RBI instructed the bank to halt onboarding new customers via its mobile application, BoB World, citing material supervisory concerns related to internal audit lapses. In May 2024, the RBI lifted these restrictions. [15] [16]

Focus[17]
The bank expects a deposit growth of 9% to 11% and a loan book growth of 11% to 13%. It aims for a margin of 2.85% to 3% for the gull year.

Subsidiary[18]
Bank has received approval from the RBI to establish a wholly owned subsidiary for undertaking Standalone Primary Dealer (SPD) business by infusing capital of up to Rs.2000 crores subject to specified conditions.

Bond Issuance[19]
Bank’s board approved raising long-term bonds up to ₹10,000 crore, in addition to ₹5,000 crore remaining from earlier approval, taking the total fundraising limit to ₹15,000 crore via infrastructure and affordable housing/green infrastructure bonds in FY26 and beyond, issued in single or multiple tranches.

Management Changes[20][21]
Mrs Mini T ceased from the position of CGM - Zonal Head on account of identification as Executive Director in Indian Bank effective 24th Nov 2025. Mrs. Archana Pandey superannuated from the position of Head Internal Audit and replaced by Mrs. Kavita Singh.
